questions.question: The number of moles of solute in 10 Liters of a 0.482 M solution is:
Which of the following undergoes intramolecular aldol condensation to give cyclohex-2-enone?
At 487 K, the equilibrium constant Kp for a reaction is 9. If the temperature is increased, favoring the endothermic direction, what happens to Kp?
questions.solution: For endothermic reactions, increasing temperature increases the equilibrium constant.

questions.question: The number of moles of solute in 9 Liters of a 0.428 M solution is:
Which of the following is most reactive towards nucleophilic addition?
10 g of a non-volatile solute (molar mass = 342 g/mol) is dissolved in 500 mL of water. Assuming ideal behaviour and solvent density of 1 g/mL, calculate the relative lowering of vapour pressure.
questions.solution: Moles solute = 10/342 = 0.029. Moles water = 500/18 = 27.78. Mole fraction = 0.00105.
